name: SL9

migrationPath.notes: One of dozens of single-tree selections; remained uncommon in cultivation
Found growing as 'Geisha Inca' — local name reflects history, not genetics

notableProducers: Genezard Perez (Vista Alegre, Amaybamba, San Fernando Valley, Peru)
description: SL9 (also SL09) is a rare cultivar belonging to the Ethiopian Legacy group, one of the dozens of single-tree selections made by Scott Agricultural Laboratories in Kenya in the 1930s. It is uncommon in cultivation today and its exact genetic fingerprint is not currently in the global database — making it a provisional designation (noted as SL9*). In Peru's Amaybamba region it is grown under the local name 'Geisha Inca,' but genetic testing confirmed it contains no Gesha in its genetic composition. This is a striking example of how local naming can diverge entirely from genetic reality. Conversations with geneticists are ongoing to determine proper variety designation.
